titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Julian Joel Obermann Papers
titlestmt.author: Mary A. Caldera

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid prepared according to Describing Archives: A Content Standard (DACS).

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday March 18, 2025 at 03:23
Finding aid revised to reflect arrangement and description of previously closed until processed material.

did.abstract: The papers consist of correspondence, draft manuscripts, teaching and committee files documenting the research, writing, editing, and teaching of Julian Joel Obermann, professor of Semitic languages at Yale University and editor of the Yale Judaica Series.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The papers are open to research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of the estate of Julian Obermann, 1959. Transfer from the holdings of the Yale Judaica Series, November, 1966.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series and one addition: I. Correspondence, 1913 - 1956. II. Presentations and lectures, circa 1930-1955. III. Writings, circa 1920-1955. IV. Academic files, 1917 - 1954. V. Personal and biographical files, 1915 - 1954.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Julian Joel Obermann (1888-1956) was born in Warsaw, Poland. He received his Ph.D., University of Vienna, 1915 and M.A. (honorary) from Yale University in 1935. Obermann emigrated to the United States in 1923. Obermann held several teaching positions: professor of Semitic languages, University of Hamburg, Germany (1919-1922?); professor of Semitic philology, Jewish Institute of Religion, New York, 1923-1931; professor, Hebrew University of Jerusalem, 1929-1930; visiting professor and professor of Semitic languages, Yale University, 1932-1956. Obermann served as co-editor of the Journal of Biblical Literature (1934-1938) and editor-in-chief of the Yale Judaica Series (1944-1956). Obermann specialized in semitic languages, Old Testament and Ugartic studies, and Islamic and Arabic culture and philosophy. He authored, edited and translated several works, including Das Problem der Kausalitaet bei den Arabern (1916); Der philosophische and religioese Subjektivismus Ghazalis (1921); The Arabic Original of Ibn Shahin's Book of Comfort (1933); Inscribed Tiles from the Synagogue of Dura (1942), Ugaritic Mythology (1948), Hugo Gressman's Tower of Babel (1928), and Soloman Gandz's translation of Maimonides' Sanctification of the New Moon (1956).
Obermann never married. He died in New Haven, Connecticut, on October 17, 1956 at the age of 68.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Julian Joel Obermann Papers (MS 330). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers consist of correspondence, lectures, presentations, writings, teaching and committee files documenting the research, writing, editing, and teaching of Julian Joel Obermann, professor of Semitic languages at Yale University and editor of the Yale Oriental Series and the Yale Judaica Series. The correspondence is primarily incoming and is both personal and professional in nature. Most of the lectures, presentations, and writings are drafts, identified and unidentified, and range from partial to complete manuscripts. The writings document Obermann's writing and editing projects, including The Arabic Original of Ibn Shahin's Book of Comfort (1933); Inscribed Tiles from the Synagogue of Dura (1942), Ugaritic Mythology (1948), Gressman's Tower of Babel (1928), The Code of Maimonides (1948-1949), and Gandz's Maimonides' Sanctification of the New Moon (1956). The bulk of the papers consist of Obermann's notebooks, which he kept for much of his career. The notebooks contain manuscript writings, lectures, and editorial and research notes. Most are not identified and have not been organized or individually described. Files on the American Oriental Society, the Committee on Judaica Research, the Judaica Research Committee, the Semitic and Biblical Club (Yale), and the Near East Languages and Literature Department (Yale), though few in number, document Obermann's leadership and interest in promoting semitic and Judaica studies. The papers also include a few legal documents, photographs, and newspaper clippings.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Archival Description Unit Processing Manual.
The 1966 Addition was previously classed as Hebrew +153; it was intergrated with the Julian Joel Papers in 2024.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The correspondence is primarily incoming and is both personal and professional in nature. A substantial amount of undated correspondence is filed at the end of the series. Additional correspondence is filed with committee files in Series IV. Academic files.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains drafts and typed copies of presentations and lectures. Additional drafts of presentations and lectures may be included in Series III. Writings, especially Notebooks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Writing series forms the bulk of the papers. It consists of manuscripts and drafts, partial and full, of Obermann's writing and editing projects, notebooks kept by Obermann, writings of others as well as unidentified manuscripts. File titles are transcribed or supplied based on the topic of the work. If known or suspected to be related, files on the same work or subject are collocated. File titles may not reflect the final titles of works documented in the series. Obermann's notebooks contain manuscript writings, lectures, and editorial and research notes. Most are not identified and have not been organized or individually described.
Files related to the The Book of Maimonides (Yale Judaica Series) is filed in Series IV under American Oriental Society Yale Judaica Series.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Obermann's writings are arranged alphabetically by title or subject, followed by unidentified Obermann (?) manuscripts, including notebooks. A small number of manuscripts writings of others is filed at the end of the series.

did.unittitle: "Alphabetic Writing: A Study in its Technical Execution"
"Calendaric Elements in the Dea Sea Scrolls"
"A Composit Inscription from the Church of St. Simeon the Stylite""

"Zur Entstehungsgeschichte des philosophischen und wissenschaftichen Stils im Neuhebraischen"
"Hebrew and Aramaic Elements in the Arabic Writings of Muhammedans"
An Important Contribution to Arabic Historiography
"Introduction A. Author and his wok B. The Manuscript C. Material for critical app."

Die Religion des Einzeluen h. des Gemeinsen (?)
"A Study in the Technique of Alphabetical Script"
"Tiles Inscribed from Dura" or "Inscribed Tiles from Dura"
"The Truth of Zion and the Reality of Palestine"
"The Two Elijah Stories in Judeo-Arabic Transmission"
Notebooks in English, German and Hebrew (some are drafts of papers, some have typescripts laid in)
Notebooks in English, German and Hebrew (some are drafts of papers, some have typescripts laid in)
Notebooks in English, German and Hebrew (some are drafts of papers, some have typescripts laid in)
Notebooks in English, German and Hebrew (some are drafts of papers, some have typescripts laid in)
Notebooks in English, German and Hebrew (some are drafts of papers, some have typescripts laid in)
Notebooks in English, German and Hebrew (some are drafts of papers, some have typescripts laid in)
"The Aramaic Incantation in Cuneiform," by Cyrus H. Gordon
"Bath-Sheba - A Drama in Three Acts," by Irma Lindheim
"The Hebrew Calendar: A Study in its Early History and Fundamental Principles" (author unknown)
"Iggeret Teman by Moses Maimonides," by Boaz Cohen
"The Lord of Wing, Water and Fire, (review) by Robert Eister
" A social and Religious History of the Jews," by Salo Wittmayer Baron
"Vocabulary of Some North Semitic Inscriptions" and "Addai, Apostle of Odessa" by George Dole

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series consists of committee, department, and teaching files. The committee and department files document Obermann's leadership and interest in promoting semitic and Judaica studies at Yale and elsewhere. The Statement of concern for German Jews and the Conference on Jewish Relations report (relation to Obermann unknown) illustrate concern about rising antisemitism and the safety of Jewish scholars. See also Series I. Correspondence, especially from 1930-1949.

did.unittitle: Committee on Arabic and Islamic Studies
Committee on Judaica Research (Yale) / Judaica Research Committee
Near East Languages and Literature Department (Yale)
Statement of concern for German Jews
The Language Institute bulletin and announcement

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains a small number of legal documents, newspaper clippings, announcements, and photographs that provide personal and biographical information on Obermann.

did.unittitle: Announcements of upcoming social events and publications (not JJO's)
Appointment and address books, business cards
List of recipients of Obermann's publications
News clippings and announcement regarding Obermann

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters to Julian Joel Obermann from various persons, with copies of his replies; for the most part in connection with his duties as editor of the Yale Judaica Series. Includes letters from O. Neugebauer, Enno Littmann, Arthur Schaade, Charles C. Torrey, Herbert Danby, G.R. Driver, and others; also letters from Isidore Epstein to Danby.

did.unittitle: Danby, Herbert, G.R. Driver, and Isidore Epstein
